page: Microsoft Azure E96ds_v7 is an Edsv7-family virtual machine configured with 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical Intel Xeon 6973P-C cores. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V, this server features 768.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. The hardware configuration includes 5669 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities, though it does not bundle dedicated GPU hardware or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong computational and memory subsystem capabilities. The Intel Xeon processor achieves a PassMark CPU score of 108,743.76 and a multi-core stress-ng score of 101,535.75, both placing in the top 25% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with small-block cached reads reaching 7,496.51 GB/sec and large-block uncached reads measured at 349.8 GB/sec. Database operations are a primary strength, evidenced by Redis SET performance reaching 159.97 million operations per second.

While the platform excels in raw throughput and prompt processing for small and medium LLM models, text generation speeds are more moderate, with the 135M model generating 171.43 tokens/sec. Additionally, the PassMark memory score sits in the middle tier despite the high raw bandwidth. This resource profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for enterprise-scale database hosting, in-memory caching, and data-intensive processing workloads that require substantial local storage and high memory density without the need for GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure E96ds_v7 is an Edsv7-family virtual machine featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs across 48 physical Intel Xeon 6973P-C cores on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of RAM, providing an 8.0 GB memory-to-vCPU ratio, and includes 5669 GB of local storage. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, this server lacks GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show top-tier performance, including a PassMark CPU score of 108,743.76, 159.97 million Redis SET operations per second, and 349.8 GB/sec uncached read memory bandwidth. However, PassMark memory scores remain average. This configuration is suited for memory-intensive databases, large-scale caching, and enterprise data processing workloads requiring substantial local storage and high-throughput memory operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96ds_v7 is a memory-optimized VM with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 5669 GB local storage, delivering top-tier database and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96ds_v7 features 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 5669 GB local storage for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 platform featuring ninety-six Intel Xeon vCPUs, extensive local storage, and high-bandwidth memory for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 768 GB of system memory
5669 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier Redis performance reaching 159.97 million operations per second
High-bandwidth memory architecture with up to 7496.51 GB/sec cached reads
Suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and data processing
